levels.label: Fragile
levels.copy: The Business Model Resilience Stress Test pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Exposed
levels.copy: Some Business Model Resilience Stress Test practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Defensible
levels.copy: Core Business Model Resilience Stress Test practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Resilient
levels.copy: Business Model Resilience Stress Test practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Adaptive
levels.copy: Business Model Resilience Stress Test is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.demand-concentration: Set customer and channel concentration thresholds and fund a second repeatable demand source.
recommendations.margin-flexibility: Model a 10%, 20%, and 30% cost shock and pre-negotiate pricing, sourcing, or scope levers.
recommendations.cash-shock-capacity: Build a 13-week downside cash plan with named triggers and decision owners.
recommendations.operational-dependencies: Map the ten critical dependencies and test recovery for the highest-impact single point of failure.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
